sections.paragraph:
φόβος, ου, ὁ [φέβομαι ‘flee in terror’]

—1. ‘feeling of need to escape from or avoid a threat’, fear J 7:13; 19:38; 20:19; Ro 8:15; 1 J 4:18. Of anxiety generated by an event or circumstance: w. focus on personal encounter 1 Cor 2:3; w. focus on transcendent influence Mt 14:26; 28:4; Mk 4:41; Rv 11:11; 18:10, 15. The idea of establishing a distance leads to

—2. ‘feeling of respect’, reverence, awe

—a. for earthly authority: ruler Ro 13:7; slave-holder 1 Pt 2:18.

—b. for divine authority or transcendent presence Ac 9:31; 19:17; Ro 3:18; 2 Cor 5:11; 7:1; Eph 5:21; 1 Pt 1:17; 3:2; Jd 23. W. focus on reaction Mt 28:8; Lk 1:12, 65; Ac 5:5; 19:17.

—c. for someone who makes inquiry about one’s beliefs reverence, courtesy 1 Pt 3:16 (but the idea in b need not be excluded).

—3. ‘the process of causing in someone a feeling of need to escape from a situation’, the act. aspect as opposed to the pass. in 1 and 2: intimidation Ro 13:3; 1 Pt 3:14.
